title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — July 2026 (v4.0 Quantitative Bridge Methodology)
summary: JULY 2026 CPI (released Aug 12, 08:30 ET). Forecast: headline 0.0% m/m (3.4% y/y; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6%); core +0.1% m/m (2.5% y/y). Key v4.0 finding: the Hormuz oil spike is real, but MONTHLY-AVERAGE arithmetic keeps July energy a DRAG — June avg retail gasoline ~$4.07/gal (EIA weekly + AAA blend) vs July 1-14 ~$3.83; retail would need to average >$4.27/gal for the rest of July to flip the energy contribution positive. The blockade premium hits the AUGUST print, not July (barring extreme escalation). Core stays soft on June's 0.0% momentum (shelter +0.1% smallest since 2021, insurance -2.0%, goods deflation). PROVISIONAL: July is 14/31 days in; the back-half oil path sets the tails. y/y INDEX-DERIVED from FRED NSA levels (Jun26 333.952 / Jul25 323.048). Jul 14 close: 10Y 4.57%, 2Y 4.14%, DXY 100.92, SPX 7545. FOMC Jul 28-29 lands before this release.
baseCase: Headline ~0.0% m/m / 3.4% y/y, core +0.1% / 2.5% — June's disinflation momentum persists in July because monthly-average gasoline stays below June's high average despite the blockade rebound; the oil spike is an August CPI story.

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: JULY 2026 print (Aug 12). Monthly-average arithmetic dominates: June avg retail gasoline ~$4.07/gal (EIA weekly Mondays 4.24/4.146/4.052/3.914/3.831 + AAA daily blend) vs July 1-14 AAA avg $3.83 (-5.8% NSA MTD, PROVISIONAL 14/31 days) — even with the Hormuz blockade rebound (EIA $3.777 Jul 6 -> $3.855 Jul 13, AAA $3.859 Jul 14, WTI ~$80), July's full-month average (~$3.90 base, -4.2%) stays below June, keeping energy a ~-0.14pp drag via beta_gas 0.95 = gasoline CPI -4.3% SA. The blockade premium hits the AUGUST print unless retail averages >$4.27 for the rest of July. Core extends June's 0.0% momentum: shelter +0.15% (run-rate 0.6/0.3/0.1 decelerating; ZORI ~+2% y/y slowest since 2020, AptList -1.2% y/y), goods ~flat (Manheim +0.1% m/m), services +0.15% (airfare pressure vs insurance deflation) -> core +0.1% / 2.5% y/y. y/y INDEX-DERIVED: headline 333.79/323.048, core 337.05/328.980 (FRED NSA). Bottom-up 3.3% y/y computed BEFORE anchors; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% overprice the back-half oil spike; moved +0.05pp documented. Prior = June actual (-0.4%/3.5%, core 0.0%/2.6%). PROVISIONAL — 14/31 days of July data.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% to -0.1% m/m, Core +0.1% m/m — monthly-average gasoline stays below June despite the blockade rebound; core momentum from June's 0.0% persists; y/y 3.3-3.4%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Choppy, range-bound tape into FOMC (Jul 28-29): 10Y 4.50-4.65%, DXY ~100-101. A soft-but-expected print keeps Sep hike pricing ~60-68% — the real repricing waits for the Aug 12 confirmation. $TLT and $GLD bid on dips; $QQQ selective with rates relief; $XLE holds the blockade premium.
scenarios.invalidation: Retail gasoline averages >$4.27/gal over the rest of July (flips energy positive), or core re-accelerates to +0.3% on airfares/tariffs.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% to +0.4% m/m, Core +0.2-0.3% m/m — extreme escalation: blockade sustains AND retail gasoline spikes >$4.30 back-half; airfares jump on jet fuel; tariff passthrough lifts goods; y/y back toward 3.6-3.8%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y spikes 4.75%+, 2Y reverses the post-CPI rally, DXY 101.5+, Sep hike >80% and a second 2026 hike prices in. Equities extend the selloff — $SPY PUT / $VIX CALL setups. $XLE and $GLD squeeze on the energy/geopolitical tail.
scenarios.invalidation: Truce re-forms and WTI collapses below $70, or the hot print is a one-off airfare spike while goods keep deflating.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.3% to -0.5% m/m, Core 0.0% m/m — Hormuz de-escalation/truce re-forms and oil gives back the spike; June's core momentum repeats (shelter +0.1%, insurance deflation, goods soft); y/y dips toward 3.1-3.2%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y drops below 4.45%, 2Y extends the post-CPI bid, DXY <100. Sep hike odds fall under 40%, late-2026 cut pricing revives. Risk-on: $QQQ +1-2%, $TLT strong, $IWM and homebuilders rally, $UAL bounces on cheaper jet fuel.
scenarios.invalidation: The blockade escalation dominates the back half of July regardless, or the market already priced the cool outcome.

setups.bias: Long duration — cool July path on monthly-average math
setups.trigger: Core stays <=+0.1% and retail gasoline avg stays <$4.00; 10Y fails 4.65%
setups.invalidation: 10Y breaks 4.75% on extreme escalation or hot core (airfares/tariffs).

setups.trigger: DXY below 101 and real yields compress; blockade persists without full escalation
setups.invalidation: DXY above 101.5 with 10Y above 4.75% kills the haven bid.

setups.bias: Cautiously long — rates relief on soft July trajectory
setups.trigger: Core momentum <=+0.1% holds and FOMC (Jul 28-29) stays on hold
setups.invalidation: Hot core or 10Y >4.75%; chip-capex headline risk.

setups.bias: Tactical long while the blockade premium holds
setups.trigger: WTI holds above $78 and Hormuz transits stay ~34/day vs 88 baseline
setups.invalidation: De-escalation sends WTI below $70 — the premium unwinds fast.

setups.bias: Avoid now; flip long on de-escalation — jet fuel elevated and rising
setups.trigger: Entry only if WTI breaks below $72 with bookings intact
setups.invalidation: Sustained blockade pushes jet fuel back toward spring peaks.

setups.bias: Protective only on the escalation tail — VIX ~15.5-17, premium not cheap
setups.trigger: VIX breakout above 20 on blockade widening or hot core repricing
setups.invalidation: Cool July trajectory + FOMC hold crushes vol back below 14.

watchItems.label: MEASURED MONTH
watchItems.note: Snapshot measures JULY price changes. Prior = June actual (Jul 14 release): headline -0.4% m/m / +3.5% y/y, core 0.0% / +2.6% (energy -5.7%, gasoline -9.7%, electricity -1.0%, utility gas +0.5%, shelter +0.1%, rent +0.1%, OER +0.2%, MV insurance -2.0%, medical svcs -0.1%, airfares +0.2%, food +0.2%).

watchItems.label: AAA/EIA Gasoline Bridge (v4.0, sourced)
watchItems.value: Jun avg ~$4.07 -> Jul 1-14 avg $3.83 (-5.8% NSA MTD)
watchItems.note: PROVISIONAL 14/31 days. Sources @2026-07-14 21:00Z: AAA daily $3.859 (7/14), $3.872 (7/13), $3.790 wk ago, $4.074 mo ago (gasprices.aaa.com). EIA weekly retail: $3.855 (Jul 13, +$0.078), $3.777 (Jul 6); June Mondays 4.146 (Jun 8), 4.052 (Jun 15), 3.914 (Jun 22), 3.831 (Jun 29) + Jun 1 ~4.24 -> June monthly avg ~$4.04 EIA / ~$4.10 AAA -> blend $4.07. Full-month base ~$3.90 (-4.2%) x beta_gas 0.95 = gasoline CPI -4.3% SA; seasonal factor assumed ~neutral, risk +/-1.5pp. Sanity bound: contribution -0.140pp within +/-20% of (0.03266 x -4.2) = [-0.166,-0.111] PASS. Flip-positive threshold: rest-of-July avg >$4.27/gal.

watchItems.label: y/y Index Anchors (v4.0, FRED)
watchItems.note: FRED CPIAUCNS retrieved 2026-07-14 21:05Z: May26 335.123, Jun26 333.952, Jul25 323.048 (base). July NSA proj ~333.79 (NSA m/m ~-0.05%, July seasonal delta ~-0.05pp vs SA) -> headline y/y 3.3%. Core: CPILFENS Jun26 336.882, Jul25 328.980 -> proj 337.05 -> core y/y 2.5%. Check: Jun26 y/y 333.952/322.561=3.5%, core 336.882/328.364=2.6% — both match BLS.

watchItems.label: Anti-Anchoring Log (v4.0)
watchItems.note: Bottom-up locked from component data (gasoline bridge + core decomposition + food) BEFORE external reads. External: prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% y/y for July (post-June repricing, Jul 14); formal July consensus not yet published (June released Jul 14); Cleveland Fed July nowcast page fetched 21:05Z — values not rendered, marked UNAVAILABLE this run. Divergence 0.2-0.3pp > 0.15pp threshold — justified component-by-component: market overprices back-half oil spike vs monthly-average arithmetic (energy -0.14pp drag; flip threshold $4.27 implausible). Final moved +0.05pp toward market on y/y (3.3->3.4% rounded); m/m unchanged. Within 0.05pp cap.

watchItems.label: Hormuz / Iran (JULY BACK-HALF RISK)
watchItems.value: US naval blockade reinstated; WTI ~$79.8, Brent ~$85
watchItems.note: Jul 14: Brent +2% $84.98, WTI +2.1% $79.79 (1-month high). 2 UAE tankers hit (1 crew dead), CENTCOM strikes 3rd night, explosions near Bandar Abbas/Kish. Trump: strait stays open, 20% transit compensation. Traffic ~34/day vs 88 baseline. SPR 316.5M bbl (lowest since 1983). Retail pass-through 1-3 weeks — path decides base vs hot tail; loads AUGUST either way (firewall: not in July point estimate beyond in-window data).

watchItems.label: Core Momentum / Shelter
watchItems.note: Shelter +0.1% (smallest since Jan 2021; run-rate Apr/May/Jun 0.6/0.3/0.1 = 0.33 avg; estimate +0.15% below run-rate on documented evidence: ZORI ~+2% y/y slowest since Dec 2020, Apartment List -1.2% y/y with vacancy 7.2% first drop in 4yrs). Motor vehicle insurance -2.0% (2nd straight big drop). Goods broadly deflating (used cars -2.0% y/y in May; Manheim Jun +0.1% m/m SA, 212.9, +2.1% y/y). Upside: airfares (jet fuel re-spike) + tariff passthrough.

watchItems.label: Fed Path / FOMC
watchItems.note: Post-June-CPI: Polymarket July hold 79.5% / hike 20.6% ($50M volume, Jul 12); 2026 hike odds ~58-60%. Waller (Jul 13): 'another higher one = signal, not noise'. Warsh testimony Jul 15. FOMC Jul 28-29 lands BEFORE this print (Aug 12) — July CPI shapes September. June jobs +57k, u3 4.2%.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST / DXY / VIX / SPX
watchItems.note: Jul 14 close: SPX +0.4% to 7545 (post-CPI relief), Nasdaq +0.9%; 10Y 4.57%, 2Y -14bp to 4.14% (biggest drop since Feb); DXY 100.92. 10Y regime line: <4.45% growth relief, >4.75% risk-off. VIX ~15.5-17 (unverified intraday).

keyDrivers: MONTHLY-AVERAGE ARITHMETIC (the v4.0 correction): June avg retail gasoline ~$4.07/gal (EIA weekly + AAA blend) vs July 1-14 $3.83 — despite the Hormuz blockade rebound (WTI ~$80, 1-month high), July's full-month average (~$3.90 base, -4.2%) stays below June. Energy remains a ~-0.14pp DRAG in July; the oil spike hits the AUGUST print unless retail averages >$4.27/gal rest-of-month.

June core momentum: core 0.0% m/m (weakest since May 2020), shelter +0.1% (smallest since 2021), insurance -2.0%, broad goods deflation. July core forecast +0.1% on persistence, capped by airfare/tariff upside.

Hormuz is a BACK-HALF path risk, not a level story: blockade reinstated, WTI 1-month high $79.8, traffic ~34/day vs 88; retail pass-through runs 1-3 weeks — decides whether July lands base or hot, and loads August either way.
y/y INDEX-DERIVED (v4.0): headline 333.79 proj / 323.048 Jul-25 base = 3.3%; core 337.05 / 328.980 = 2.5%. Prior run's 322.878 base corrected to FRED-actual 323.048.

Anti-anchoring: bottom-up 0.0% m/m / 3.3% y/y computed 21:05Z BEFORE external anchors; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% overprice the back-half spike; documented +0.05pp move only; Cleveland Fed nowcast unavailable this run.
Fed: July hike ~20% (Polymarket), September ~60-68% — this print (Aug 12) lands directly in the September pricing window; FOMC Jul 28-29 precedes the release.
Strong USD (DXY 100.92) keeps core-goods import deflation working against tariff passthrough (nonfuel imports +0.8% m/m in May).
PROVISIONAL / capped confidence 55: July is 14/31 days in, vol regime active (gasoline MTD move -5.8% >5%, active supply shock) — scenario span 0.9pp, no tail below 20%.
risks: Extreme escalation (top risk): blockade widens or strikes hit Gulf infrastructure — retail gasoline >$4.30 back-half flips July energy positive and invalidates the cool/base call.
Core re-acceleration: airfares on the jet-fuel spike plus tariff passthrough could push core to +0.2-0.3%.
PROVISIONAL data risk: 14/31 days; the back-half oil path swings the energy component ~0.2pp either way.
Consensus convergence: post-June repricing already moved markets (Sep hike ~60-68%); a merely-soft July print may be 'priced' by Aug 12, dulling the rally.
PPI pipeline: July PPI (~Aug 13) and June import prices (Jul 15) test the goods-deflation assumption.
Warsh testimony (Jul 15) and Waller's hawkish signal: a front-end repricing before the data lands.
Nowcast blind spot: Cleveland Fed July nowcast unavailable this run — cross-check next run; if it prints materially above 3.4% y/y, re-run the divergence test.
methodology: v4.0 two-stage aggregation, exact BLS weights. ENERGY BRIDGE: EIA weekly + AAA monthly averages — June ~$4.07 vs July 1-14 $3.83 (-5.8% NSA MTD, PROVISIONAL 14/31 days); full-month base ~$3.90 (-4.2%) x beta_gas 0.95 = gasoline CPI -4.3% SA. Sanity bound PASS. Core DECOMPOSED: shelter +0.15%, core goods +0.05%, core services +0.15% -> core +0.127% via /0.80557. y/y INDEX-DERIVED from FRED NSA levels. ANTI-ANCHORING: bottom-up BEFORE external anchors; +0.05pp documented move. June 2026 actuals (Jul 14): headline -0.4%/3.5%, core 0.0%/2.6%.

aggregation.componentSources: AAA daily national avg (Jul 1-14 avg $3.83, $3.859 on 7/14, PROVISIONAL); EIA weekly retail ($3.777 Jul 6 -> $3.855 Jul 13; June Mondays 4.146/4.052/3.914/3.831 -> Jun avg ~$4.07 blend); BLS shelter run-rate Apr/May/Jun 0.6/0.3/0.1; Zillow ZORI ~+2% y/y; Apartment List -1.2% y/y (vacancy 7.2%); Manheim Jun +0.1% m/m SA (212.9, +2.1% y/y); FRED CPIAUCNS/CPILFENS NSA index levels (retrieved 2026-07-14 21:05Z)
aggregation.verification: Food(13.25)+Energy(6.193)+Core(80.557)=100.000 | Shelter(34.411)+CoreGoods(18.4)+CoreServices(27.746)=80.557 | Core identity: (0.0516+0.0092+0.0416)/0.80557=0.127% | Energy sanity bound: -0.140pp within +/-20% of (0.03266 x -4.2)=[-0.166,-0.111] PASS | Energy sub-sum: -0.1403+0.0046+0.0011+0.0003-0.0009=-0.1352 = energyContribution PASS | July inputs PROVISIONAL (14/31 days)
